Setting up a payment processor to handle your transactions. You want people to pay you in absolutely any way possible and the way I like to look at it is the least friction possible. You want the least barrier barrier entry for somebody to give you money. So there's options like PayPal. PayPal is one of the most easy to use sites and you can embed it into your website and I don't care what you're selling. PayPal is absolutely the fastest and smoothest transaction until you start getting into huge amounts of revenue. Credit card wise you can use Square or PayPal has an attachment as well and you put that right at the top of your phone or iPad and now if you're a barber or hairdresser, a makeup artist, a masseuse, you sell grapefruits and lemonade, doesn't matter what you sell, you can attach this little square to the top of your phone or the little PayPal transaction one and anywhere you are the people can just swipe right there. So you want to again, you don't want to be massaging somebody or being out there selling lemonade or selling baseball cards or doing something that's a person a person like being a barber and you're and they all of a sudden I don't have cash on me I just have a credit card. Well you're in luck. I have my Square Reader or my PayPal Reader right here and they can just swipe right there on the spot. Another great one online is called Stripe. Stripe is one of the most impressive companies to come out over the last few years and it handles the transactions for you for a very affordable rate. There's a lot of great processors through the banks. Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Chase, most of the banks have very very good processing at low affordable rates and they direct deposit directly into your account. You want people to pay you with Bitcoin then you can use somebody like gocoin.com and they can pay you with Bitcoin. All these things are pretty easy to set up. You just have to have your corporation and trademark and bank account set up beforehand so that they can connect those things together. But you want to get every way that people can pay you. Stripe, Square, PayPal, Cash, Checks, Wire, all those things. You want to make it very very readily available so that no matter what people want to do and however they want to pay you you have a way for that to happen. So make sure you get your corporation, get your bank account, get your tax ID number. You have to have all those things in order to set up with the payment processors and research if there's certain payment processors that fit you I recommend using multiple. You want to be with PayPal, Stripe and Square if you can and just taking money any any different way shape or form.
